name: Dike
description: Dike, in Greek mythology, is the goddess of justice, fairness, and moral order. She is one of the Horae, daughters of Zeus and Themis. Dike is often depicted as a young woman carrying scales, symbolizing impartiality, and a sword, representing punishment for injustice. She is a staunch advocate for righteous behavior and punishes those who deviate from the path of justice. Her role is to maintain balance and harmony in the world, ensuring that individuals receive what they deserve according to natural and moral law. Dike is concerned with both human and divine justice, observing the actions of mortals and gods alike, reporting wrongdoing to Zeus for appropriate judgment. She is closely associated with law and order, acting as a guardian against deceit, corruption, and oppression. Dike's influence extends to all aspects of life, from personal conduct to societal structures, emphasizing the importance of integrity and accountability. She ensures that hubris is punished and that those who uphold justice are rewarded. Dike is a powerful figure who embodies the fundamental principles of fairness and equity, serving as a constant reminder of the importance of upholding moral standards.
subtitle: Goddess of Justice and Fairness

keyMyths.title: The Departure of Astraea
keyMyths.description: As humanity descended into the Iron Age and wickedness prevailed, Dike's sister, Astraea, the goddess of innocence, fled the earth, symbolizing the loss of virtue. Dike remained longer, but ultimately also departed due to the overwhelming injustice.

keyMyths.title: Dike and Zeus
keyMyths.description: Dike reports instances of injustice to Zeus, who then takes action to restore balance and punish wrongdoers. This highlights her role as an intermediary between the mortal realm and the divine authority responsible for upholding justice.

keyMyths.title: The Punishment of Hubris
keyMyths.description: Dike relentlessly pursues those who commit acts of hubris, excessive pride or arrogance, ensuring that they face consequences for their actions. This reinforces the importance of humility and respect for the natural order.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Contains early references to Dike and her role in maintaining justice.
sources.text: Contains early references to Dike and her role in maintaining justice.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: Describes the departure of Astraea and Dike from the earth.
sources.text: Describes the departure of Astraea and Dike from the earth.

sources.author: Various Greek Tragedians
sources.description: Illustrate Dike's role in punishing hubris and upholding moral order.
